title: About skill consumers - Bot Service
display_title: Design and manage skill consumer (root) bots
description: Describes how conversational logic in a skill bot can be used by a skill consumer using the Bot Framework SDK.
summary:
APPLIES TO:SDK v4 Askill consumeris a bot that can call one or more skills.
With respect to skills, aroot botis a user-facing bot that is also a skill consumer. From the user's perspective, the root bot is the bot they're interacting with.
From the skill's perspective, the skill consumer is the channel over which it communicates with the user.
(For more information, see theskills overview.) As a skill consumer, a root bot includes some additional logic to manage traffic between it and a skill:
classification_reason: Explains how root/skill consumer bots manage traffic with skills; this is a concrete Bot Framework architectural pattern for multi-bot composition.

title: Skills overview - Bot Service
display_title: Architect and reuse skills across Bot Framework bots
description: Describes the concepts of how conversational logic in one bot can be used by another bot using the Bot Framework SDK.
summary:
APPLIES TO:SDK v4 You can extend a bot using askillbot.
A skill can be consumed by various other bots, facilitating reuse, and in this way, you can create a user-facing bot and extend it by consuming your own or third-party skills. In other words, the user interacts directly with the root bot, and the root bot delegates some of its conversational logic to a skill. The skills feature is designed so that: This diagram shows some of the possible permutations.
classification_reason: Skills overview describes how one bot’s conversational logic is reused by others; this is a Bot Framework–specific architectural pattern for composing bots.

toc_title: Debug a skill or skill consumer
title: How to debug a skill or skill consumer - Bot Service
display_title: Debug Bot Framework skills and consumers remotely
description: Describes how to debug a skill or skill consumer with Dev Tunnels, when only one bot is local.
summary: APPLIES TO:SDK v4 You may need to debug a skill or skill consumer bot, where the one you want to debug is running locally and the other is running in the cloud. In this situation, you can use the Dev Tunnel tool to expose the local bot endpoint as a public URL.
classification_reason: Describes how to debug when either the skill or consumer is local and the other is in the cloud, using Dev Tunnels; this is a product-specific diagnostic scenario.

title: Bot Framework a skill manifest schema - Bot Service
display_title: Author Bot Framework skill manifest JSON
description: Learn about the contents and structure of a Bot Framework skill manifest.
summary: APPLIES TO:SDK v4 Askill manifestis a JSON file that describes the actions a skill can take, its input and output parameters, and the skill's endpoints. The manifest contains machine-readable information a developer can use to access the skill from another bot. This article describes supported versions of the Bot Framework skill manifest schema. The Bot Framework skill manifest schemas usedraft 7of the JSON schema vocabulary.
classification_reason: Documents the skill manifest schema, including JSON properties, versions, and structure, which are precise configuration parameters unique to Bot Framework skills.

toc_title: Add trace activities to your bot
title: Add trace activities to your bot in Bot Framework SDK - Bot Service
display_title: Use trace activities to debug bots locally
description: Learn what the trace activity is and how to use it to view information about your bot while it runs locally.
summary:
APPLIES TO:SDK v4 Atrace activityis an activity that your bot can send to the Bot Framework Emulator.
You can use trace activities to interactively debug a bot, as they allow you to view information about your bot while it runs locally. Trace activities are sent only to the Emulator and not to any other client or channel.

classification_reason: Explains the trace activity type and how it is sent only to the Emulator, including Bot Framework–specific activity configuration and debugging behavior.
